top_signals.title: Oil Dips After Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Even as Clashes Persist
top_signals.summary: Oil declined on hopes that the US and Iran are inching closer to a peace deal after a conditional ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, even as the truce was marred by ongoing clashes.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Iran's Oil Exports Collapse to Six-Year Low as Blockade Tightens
top_signals.summary: Iran's oil exports fell to their lowest level in at least six years in May as the U.S. naval blockade continued to choke off crude shipments and leave tens of millions of barrels stranded at sea. According to shipping data from Vortexa, Iran exported just 209,000 barrels per day (bpd) of crude oil and condensate in May, down from 1.34 million bpd in April and nearly 1.9 million bpd in March. Kpler had estimated May exports slightly higher at 260,000 bpd, but still the lowest level since the height of the Trump administration's "maximum pressure"…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: This Jobs Report Will Not Help the Fed, Says JPM's Berro
top_signals.summary: Kelsey Berro, fixed income portfolio manager at JPMorgan Asset Management, joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." She says the May monthly jobs report won't help the Federal Reserve because it's still so concerned about the inflation outlook. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Statistical Notice 2026/05 - Implementation of Bank of England Statistics Taxonomy v1.3.1
top_signals.summary: Statistical Notices update the definitions and guidance contained in the Banking Statistics Yellow Folder
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
top_signals.summary:
CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation.

  • CVE-2022-0492 Linux Kernel Improper Authentication Vulnerability
  • CVE-2025-48595 Android Framework Integer Overflow Vulnerability

These types of vulnerabilities are a frequent attack vector for malicious cyber actors and pose significant risks to the federal enterprise.

Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01: Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities established the KEV Catalog as a living list of known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that carry significant risk to the federal enterprise. BOD 22-01 requires Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to remediate identified vulnerabilities by the due date to protect FCEB networks against active threats. See the BOD 22-01 Fact Sheet for more information.

Although BOD 22-01 only applies to FCEB agencies, CISA strongly urges all organizations to reduce their exposure to cyberattacks by prioritizing timely remediation of KEV Catalog vulnerabilities as part of their vulnerability management practice. CISA will continue to add vulnerabilities to the catalog that meet the specified criteria.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Potential liquidity or banking-system instability affecting financial conditions.

top_signals.summary: Nvidia and Anthropic Beat Back June Gloom  WSJ
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
